description_long: This house features a simple rectangular structure with a gable roof, finished in a light, concrete-like material. The front facade includes a single door and a set of four-pane windows, while the rear side has two large rectangular windows. The side walls are mostly solid, with one side completely blank and the other divided into two contrasting panels. Subtle surface cracks and faint lines are visible on the exterior, suggesting a weathered or unfinished look. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible decorative elements, porches, or overhangs, and the windows and door are framed in a straightforward, utilitarian style.
description: A minimalistic, rectangular house with a gable roof, light concrete-like exterior, and simple window and door arrangements. The design is plain and functional, with subtle surface cracks and no decorative features.
description_view_0: The front view shows a door on the left and a four-pane window on the right, both framed simply against a light, slightly cracked wall.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the gable end of the roof and a plain, unadorned wall with no windows or doors.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a solid wall divided into two panels, one darker and one lighter, with no windows or doors.
description_view_3: The right side view features two large rectangular windows, symmetrically placed under the gable roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple flush door with no steps, porch, or decorative elements
